title: faulthandler: is_enabled() reads the global fatal_error.enabled flag that enable()/disable() write without synchronization
notes: is_enabled() (faulthandler.c:686, `return fatal_error.enabled;`) is another unsynchronized reader of the non-atomic `enabled` flag that enable() (:538) / disable() write. Same process-global _PyRuntime.faulthandler state as the already-reported enable()/disable() race (python/cpython#151363; the related watchdog lock-handshake race is #151475). Cataloged as known so fleets dedupe it rather than resurfacing it as new. Not separately filed; if filing follow-up, note is_enabled() as an additional racing accessor.
fleet02_notes: fleet-02: additional signature face(s) folded in: Modules/faulthandler.c:faulthandler_disable | Modules/faulthandler.c:faulthandler_is_enabled_impl
